accessibilityRules.requirement: Not focusable, not announced by screen readers — purely visual layout

doRules: Use ToolbarSpacer between logical groups of toolbar actions to push subsequent items to the trailing edge
One spacer per Toolbar is usually sufficient
Place before trailing actions (Refresh, More, Emphasized primary) so left-side items stay left-aligned
dontRules: Do not use ToolbarSpacer outside a Toolbar / OverflowToolbar / Bar
Do not stack multiple spacers to fake centering — use Toolbar's own alignment options
Do not use with an explicit width — SAP spacer is a flex grower, not a fixed gap

pluginNotes.sizingConstraints: Not a real Figma component. Plugin sets layoutGrow=1 on the PREVIOUS sibling so it stretches, pushing subsequent siblings to the trailing edge.
knownIssues: ToolbarSpacer is a plugin-side layout marker, not a SAP library component. It has no renderable Figma node — the effect is achieved by setting layoutGrow=1 on the previous sibling in the toolbar. See plugin code.js line ~2200.
